review_text: Poca cosa más si vas de camino a Flindees y quieres dormir.

Fantastic caravan park with true Aussie spirit. Len and Bev are marvellous and the happy hours and sausage sizzles are a welcomed treat. We loved our 6 nights there. Big, flat sites, some grassed and under trees if you want. Spotless amenities.

What a great place to stay if you want something a bit different from other parks. Very quiet amongst rural settings. Caretakers Len and Bev couldn’t be nicer but leave you on your own if you choose to.
Very relaxed “ happy hour” where you are not obliged to go but a really casual atmosphere if you do.
Sites well planned and quite historic location in the old school.
However if you go in summer or thereabouts you should bring fly net,  as flies pretty bad.
The amenities are very good with newish facilities and great laundry.
The only complaint, has nothing to do with park but to do with the attitude of new owner of pub who apparently only opens at his choosing. So as long as one doesn’t come here with preconceived notion of a friendly and available pub around the corner, definitely the park to go to for a bit of quiet time.
( little tip, if you are in caravan don’t go through Quorn to get there, as it’s all dirt, you need to go up from Orroroo or down from Hawker )

What a great place! Grassed sites, clean amenities super hot hot water. The have laundry and camp kitchen also. Great people running the place and they do happy hour as well.
and cheap $38 glad we drove the extra from Hawker to come here.

We were assigned 'The Library'. A comfortable room, most comfortable bed I have slept in. Exceptional hospitality. So much history here in what was a rural school. If we had the time, we would  have stayed longer. A quiet location and plenty of opportunity to take a leisurely walk through the town.

We stayed at this lovely caravan park for 5 nights. We met Len and Bev (the caretakers) such a lovely couple that have gone above and beyond to make the park as welcoming as possible. They hold a happy hr 4pm You take your own drink of choice and Len & Bev provide the snacks. It really is one of the best parks we have ever stayed at.   We will certainly return.

Awesome little community caravan park with friendly and helpful caretakers. Water, electricity and laundry available. Has a mixture of grass and gravel sites and a rustic camp kitchen. Would definitely stay there again.
Great little park, convenient between Broken Hill and Flinders Ranges. Good sites, amenities and very hospitable hosts. Lovely chats at daily happy hour in a cosy indoor lounge setting.

What a fabulous little caravan park and Bev & Len are great hosts and make you feel like family. Nothing is too much trouble.
Happy hour every afternoon with Bev & Len providing homemade goodies. Lovely relaxed atmosphere sitting around the fire, chatting & sharing stories with other travellers. Amenities are clean with nice hot showers & bath mats provided.
Thank you Bev, Len & not to forget little Charlie for a wonderful 4 day stay.
Lynn & David

The people running it were great. Very keen and helpful. We were there in August. It was so crowded and full. The camp kitchen could only be described as ok. The toilet stall I was using had no paper. Yikes. We were in the unpowered area. Someone who arrived later was too close to us. Unfortunately the spots are not clearly marked. One of the vans was discharging gray water into the grass and not into a drain. Fire pit remains where just dumped in the garden. Ughh. The unpowered area was on green grass which is good. And it has some character. I suppose I don't like the showers being seperate from the toilets. Others in the group liked the fresh foot mats in the showers..

Had a great 5 nights stay here with 5 other friends in our caravans. Len and Bev were great hosts. The afternoon catchup was good with nibbles and cosy fire. Met some other lovely caravanners. Bev look after Charlie or I'll be back for him..... regards Janet, Trev and Boss
Fantastic spot to stay.

Len and Bev are great hosts. The amenities were spotless. The setting was peaceful and happy hour felt like a family get together complete with homemade quiches and other nibbles. Dogs were all welcome and our dog Buddy had the best time playing with his new friends. Look forward to another time we can stay there.
Colleen and Mars and Buddy

A gem of a find - a great place to stay, with amazing volunteer hosts. We all loved our stay in Carrieton - a pretty location and a gorgeous park. The communal happy hour in the evening was lovely - and we got to get tips from other travellers. Amenities block was clean, drinking water was good, camp kitchen was reasonable (but pretty chilly as it was outside). Our family stayed in the bunkhouse - it was warm and comfortable, with lots of room for all of us. The beds were reasonably comfortable, and the quilts were warm. There were clean sheets, pillows, and towels. We couldn't have asked for more.
Lovely managers. Great park

Only a small park but has options of drive through sites and grassy sites, really great that the community has turned the old school into a caravan park. And as usual the big bonus for us is that it is dog friendly.

Had a wonderful weekend in Carrieton, we were there for a family reunion. Len and Bev and all the towns folk went above and beyond our expectations.
Lovely caravan park and being well. Are for by Len and Bev.
Great little park with lovely green grass. Fabulous managers. Thank you.

Stayed two nights and Was welcomed by Len when we drove in and showed us our grassy site. Checked out the amenities and they are great showers and toilets and spotlessly clean. Happy hour  was amazing with Len and Bev providing home made sausage rolls also spring rolls chips and dips and we also had a great chat with other guests. Bev gave us a personal tour of the old school buildings that have been turned into accommodation. We loved the warm welcoming atmosphere and will certainly be back.
Peter and Glenda
Adequate without being exciting I was happy to stay there o/night but I don't think it would be a good place to holiday.
Excellent little community run caravan park,  had an great night at the Rodeo
Spent a Wednesday night there,sausage sizzle night raising money for a playground. Friendly hosts Leonard and Bev made everyone welcome..

We recently stayed here, for 10 nights (Travel Auctions). Len and Bev make you feel very, very welcome. The old Primary School has been transformed into a great little community owned caravan park. 'Happy Hour' takes on a whole new meaning, with Bev's sausage rolls and garlic breads.

Lovely quiet location
The staff were so helpful and welcoming
The cottage was lovely clean and cosy

What a great little find, we will definitely be using this little Gem of a Caravan Park more often now whilst travelling in the Flinders. A VERY Quiet park is greatly appreciated by us, Amenities are VERY Clean & a Dog Friendly park. We only wanted to stay one night but ended up staying two.
Well Done & Thankyou for the lovely Quiet stay.

We recently spent one night at this lovely caravan park and we couldn’t recommend it highly enough. The volunteer hosts were lovely and even served us warm sausage rolls. Clean amenities, comfy beds, and a welcoming atmosphere. Will definitely stay here again!
Loved the stay , will stay again soon!!!!!
Great little van Park in this beautiful old sandstone town.  Friendly caretaker.  Green grass.  Right near pub that is open again too.  Recommended.

Value for money, it was the best from my motorcycle 4500k round trip.
$45 bucks for the budget bunk room, jeez some places would charge that for a powered site.  For a motorcyclist camping out on long trips, this beats the heck outta setting up a tent, equipment etc.
I just unpack wash and sleep! Picture shows a few hundred words.
Martin, the caretaker was friendly and efficient; facilities basic but efficient and clean. Has to be a stop for my next long trip

Stayed on our way to Flinders Ranges. Basic caravan park, but excellent value for money. Hosts were fantastic and it is the people you meet at these small parks that make travelling so rewarding. Enjoyed a ‘get together’ in the Park hosted by the managers and they are able to order great meals into the park if you organise in advance, to save you cooking. Would definitely stay again on our next trip.

Very happy with this park and would recommend it to passing travelers but only Telstra reception so keep that in mind . Len and Bev who run the very clean park are perfect hosts and the nice large area of lawn is a treat to camp on in a very dry region .

We spent 10 days at the park with our AVAN, my wife and I with our 2 Jack Russells. Dogs had a ball, we had a great rest. Caretakers Len and Bev are great people and the happy hours at 4pm were enjoyable.

I can't speak highly enough both of the folks and the facilities.  Len was so accommodating and friendly. The caravan park has everything you need - clean showers and toilets, rainwater camp kitchen, powered sites, kids playground and even a cricket pitch. Best experience ever - we even decided to stay another night.

I have stayed in Caravan Parks all over Australia. This tiny little community owned Park, has the cleanest facilities I have ever seen. We were welcomed with a plate of homemade sausage rolls by Bev and Len, the volunteer managers. If I could give them six stars, I would.
A big shout out to Bev and Les who made us feel so welcome at this well appointed campsite with rich thick grass on which to pitch our tent for an overnight stay.

My wife and I stayed at the park for 10 nights on a site. Absolutely fantastic stay. The park managers Len and Bev were most accommodating and looked after all our needs. We will definitely go to Carrieton Caravan Park again and will definitely recommend it to others.

Historic village to go wandering
The Park was peaceful little grass shady trees
4pm happy hour Bev supplied nibbles,  interesting chats with other campers.
Hotel close by, but only opens Friday to Sunday, so no  review on such.

The couple managing the caravan park are great, and easy to get along with and easy to talk to.  Happy hour at 4 pm in the 'office'/rec room. |Plenty of space for the caravan or camper, plus, the old school buildings have been converted into sleeping quarters and conference facilities. A great 👍  destination, not just a stopover

What a gorgeous place to relax and unwind. Grassed sites, camp kitchen,  hot showers,  lovely gardens.  The caretakers Bev and Len were so welcoming and such nice people. They set up an afternoon get together with nibbles and a wood fire, to sit around and chat about adventures and travels. Pet friendly with a dump point. Powered and unpowered sites, washing machine and drier. Budget rooms and a large room for groups. Genuine country hospitality. Don't miss out on this wonderful place and meeting Bev and Len. The town is walking distance,  a pub for grub, and the local shop offers everything to cook for yourself on the bbq in the camp kitchen. Petrol available as well in the town
Very welcoming, great caretaker, great facilities, firewood available and fantastic pub

We chose to stay at the Carrieton Caravan Park specifically because it is community owned and run. Located in the Old school grounds the Old school House and teachers cottage offer fabulous accommodation facilities.
We stayed in the park with our camper, unpowered - $20 for 2.
Powered sites are available.
The amenities are spotless and modern.
There is a laundry as well as a camp kitchen (with great bbq), a lounge with woodburner. There is a dump point in the park.
Carrieton is a small town - just 46 pop. But there is a well stocked community general store selling fuel as well (good prices too).
The refurbished pub a short walk from the park offers great meals and a chilled atmosphere.
We booked in for one night and stayed 5.
Close to many other points of interest.
We'll def go back!
Loved this quiet friendly caravan park. Clean facilities and a 2 ply toilet paper park.
